extraction.one_line_summary: MAS Enric attacked LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel on the final corners of the Alto de Balcón de Bizkaia, studying Valverde's 2012 victory tape to time his sprint perfectly.
tactical_patterns: YATES Simon attacked when VALVERDE couldn't respond, knowing repeated visual presence of rival creates psychological pressure regardless of legs.
MAS Enric pre-studied 2012 finish footage to identify exact corner where early sprint would close door on LÓPEZ before final straight.
QUINTANA Nairo deliberately waited for VALVERDE rather than following moves, suggesting tactical collaboration to protect Spanish GC positions.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Dominated both final mountain stage and prior day, showing Grand Tour winners can peak twice when first attempt (Giro) ends badly.
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: Tactically sophisticated beyond age, using video study to execute precise corner-based attack that neutralized LÓPEZ's superior sprint.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: positive: Aggressive GC move trying to overcome 31-second deficit on penultimate stage shows willingness to gamble when mathematics require it.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: Lost 32 seconds immediately when YATES attacked, then bled time to lose nearly 4.5 minutes, indicating complete form collapse.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: neutral: Dropped back without apparent physical distress to pace VALVERDE, sacrificing own result for team GC protection.
